rationale: Ground truth shows a clean, low-risk change verified by a human (boundary check, unit tests, L4 regression all passed) and merged with two LGTMs — no real defects existed. X stays disciplined, validates every hunk in the actual diff, and offers only minor/nit suggestions (missing positive boundary test, hardcode-vs-derive message) that are grounded and consistent with that outcome. Y reaches REQUEST_CHANGES on the strength of two files entirely outside the diff (tests/dfx/reliability/...) with suspiciously precise fabricated-looking line numbers and code that contradict the PR's actual clean-merge outcome and the human's 'related unit tests passed' verification — an unverifiable, high-confidence claim that reads as hallucination and sharply hurts precision despite otherwise solid, accurate 'what I validated' analysis. Both give concrete file/line actionable feedback, but X's is trustworthy while Y's headline finding is not.
